name: Yogurt, plain (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (plain, whole-milk cow yogurt; ~100 g ≈ 100 mL, density ~1.03 g/mL)

components.name: lactose
components.source: USDA FDC 171284 (total sugars 4.66 g/100g) + Marshall/Cheng HPLC J Dairy Sci 1987 70:doi:10.3168/jds.S0022-0302(87)80124-8
components.evidence: HPLC: residual lactose ~3.70 g/100g after storage in plain yogurt; consistent with USDA total sugars 4.66 g/100g partitioned lactose+galactose. Dominant residual sugar; MW 342.3.

components.name: D-galactose
components.source: HPLC J Dairy Sci 1987 doi:10.3168/jds.S0022-0302(87)80124-8; total-galactose review Food Chem/IJDT S0958-6946(17)30137-1
components.evidence: Galactose accumulates during fermentation (S. thermophilus / L. bulgaricus are largely galactose-negative): 0.88 g/100g post-incubation rising to ~1.04 g/100g after 50 d. Free galactose is a key fermentable released by lactose hydrolysis. MW 180.16.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Glucose held at trace levels throughout fermentation/storage (preferentially consumed by starter bacteria while galactose accumulates). Trace value; MW 180.16.

components.name: L-lactic acid
components.source: Fernandez-Garcia & McGregor 1994 J Dairy Sci 77:2934-2939 (PMID 7836580); fermentation/shelf-life reviews (range 0.6-1.5%)
components.evidence: Lactic acid is the DOMINANT organic acid and a major carbon/energy metabolite of yogurt (titratable acidity ~0.9% lactic; regulatory minimum 0.6%). Midpoint ~0.9 g/100g, range 0.6-1.5 g/100g; MW 90.08. Was entirely MISSING from the FooDB profile.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: Residual citrate ~2.3 mg/g (230 mg/100g); native milk citrate partly consumed during fermentation. MW 192.12. MISSING from FooDB profile.

components.name: acetic acid
components.evidence: Acetic acid increases during yogurt fermentation; present as a minor secondary fermentation acid (typically tens of mg/100g). Unquantified here; included as a genuine growth-relevant metabolite.

components.name: orotic acid
components.evidence: Native dairy pyrimidine acid (milk ~5-10 mg/100g); increases slightly in yogurt. Trace, unquantified; genuine dairy metabolite.

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: Total protein 3.47 g/100g (mainly casein + whey); N source. Amino-acid profile below.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: Protein-bound amino-acid profile (was missing from FooDB current list).

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: Protein-bound amino-acid profile (was missing from FooDB current list).

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Protein-bound amino-acid profile (glutamic acid); most abundant amino acid.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Whole-milk yogurt total lipid 3.25 g/100g (milk fat).

components.name: palmitic acid (C16:0)
components.evidence: Most abundant fatty acid in milk fat.

components.name: oleic acid (C18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Dominant monounsaturated FA; total MUFA 0.893 g/100g, largely oleic.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (C18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Minor n-3 milk fatty acid within total PUFA 0.092 g/100g.

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: Genuine animal sterol of dairy fat (KEPT: yogurt is an animal food, so cholesterol is real, not FooDB noise).

components.name: iodine
components.evidence: Genuine dairy ultratrace element; present, unquantified in USDA SR entry.

components.name: riboflavin (B2)
components.evidence: Yogurt is a notable riboflavin source.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Genuine dairy B-vitamin (~2-4 µg/100g typical); unquantified in USDA SR entry.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Genuine trace carotenoid of milk fat (gives dairy fat its color); KEPT as minor, unquantified.

remove.name: Isoflavones
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone class; implausible in plain dairy yogurt (belongs to SOY yogurt). FooDB matrix contamination.

remove.name: Genistin
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone glycoside; plant compound, implausible in dairy yogurt.

remove.name: Genistein
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone aglycone; plant compound, implausible in dairy yogurt.

remove.name: Daidzin
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone glycoside; not a dairy nutrient.

remove.name: Daidzein
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone; not a dairy nutrient.

remove.name: Glycitin
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone glycoside; not a dairy nutrient.

remove.name: Glycitein
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone; not a dairy nutrient.

remove.name: 6''-Malonylgenistin
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone conjugate; not a dairy nutrient.

remove.name: 6''-O-Acetylgenistin
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone conjugate; not a dairy nutrient.

remove.name: 6''-Acetyldaidzin
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone conjugate; not a dairy nutrient.

remove.name: 6''-Malonyldaidzin
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone conjugate; not a dairy nutrient.

remove.name: 6''-Malonylglycitin
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone conjugate; not a dairy nutrient.

remove.name: 6''-Acetylglycitin
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone conjugate; not a dairy nutrient.

remove.name: Formononetin
remove.reason: Legume isoflavone (soy/clover); implausible in dairy yogurt.

remove.name: Coumestrol
remove.reason: Legume coumestan phytoestrogen; implausible in dairy yogurt.

remove.name: Secoisolariciresinol
remove.reason: Plant lignan (flax/soy); implausible in dairy yogurt.

remove.name: Lariciresinol
remove.reason: Plant lignan; implausible in dairy yogurt.

remove.name: (+)-Pinoresinol
remove.reason: Plant lignan; implausible in dairy yogurt.

remove.name: (-)-Matairesinol
remove.reason: Plant lignan; implausible in dairy yogurt.

remove.name: Caffeine
remove.reason: Methylxanthine from coffee/tea; implausible in plain yogurt.

remove.name: Theobromine
remove.reason: Cocoa methylxanthine; implausible in plain yogurt.

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: Furanocoumarin from Apiaceae/citrus plants; implausible in dairy yogurt.

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: Trace aroma aldehyde, not a growth nutrient; negligible non-nutritive volatile.

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: Added antimicrobial food preservative (would inhibit, not feed, microbes); not a native yogurt nutrient.

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Stereochemistry artifact / duplicate of L-isoleucine already reported; not a distinct nutrient.

remove.name: 24,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D3
remove.reason: Predicted trace vitamin D catabolite; negligible and not a growth nutrient.

remove.name: beta-Lactose
remove.reason: Anomer of lactose already reported as 'lactose'; redundant duplicate, not an additional sugar.

missing_added: L-lactic acid (0.9 g/100g, dominant organic acid — was entirely absent)
specific sugars with real amounts: lactose 3.7, D-galactose 1.0, D-glucose trace
L-phenylalanine and L-valine (missing from FooDB amino-acid list)
quantitative minerals and vitamins from USDA FDC 171284
keep_uncovered: beta-carotene (genuine trace milk-fat carotenoid, no growth role but real)
Myristoleic acid (C14:1, genuine minor milk fatty acid)
Nonanoate / gadoleic acid (C9:0, C20:1 — genuine trace odd/long-chain milk fatty acids)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 171284 (Yogurt, plain, whole milk) — macros, minerals, vitamins, full amino-acid and fatty-acid panel, cholesterol
Marshall/Cheng et al., Determination of Sugars in Yogurt by HPLC, J Dairy Sci 1987 70, doi:10.3168/jds.S0022-0302(87)80124-8 — residual lactose/galactose/glucose
Fernandez-Garcia E & McGregor JU 1994, Determination of organic acids during fermentation and cold storage of yogurt, J Dairy Sci 77:2934-2939, PMID 7836580 — lactic/citric/acetic/orotic acids
Fermented-milk galactose review, Int Dairy J / Food Chem, doi:10.1016/j.idairyj.2017.06.001 (total galactose 2.3-2.4 g/100g)
FooDB FOOD00620 (Yogurt) — base record; noise flagged for removal
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for microbial growth on plain yogurt are lactic acid (~0.9 g/100g), lactose (~3.7 g/100g) and free galactose (~1.0 g/100g); glucose stays at trace because starter bacteria consume it while galactose accumulates. Organic acids (lactic + citric) were completely missing from the FooDB profile and are the key correction. Removed a large block of soy isoflavones/plant lignans plus methylxanthines that are FooDB matrix contamination for plain dairy yogurt; cholesterol and milk-fat carotenoids are KEPT because yogurt is an animal food.
